name: jsonl-optional-ts-absent-omits-key
comment: Regression: `ts` is optional in the protocol. TS reads entry.timestamp, so an absent timestamp is undefined and JSON.stringify omits the key; Python built the same event as a dict and serialized "ts": null, putting a key on the wire the TS server never sends. protocol.py's whole reason for keeping ChatEvent a plain dict is byte-for-byte parity with the TS server, so this is pinned here rather than left to a docs note.
